channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How Trump is blocking U.S. states from regulating artificial intelligence
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday aimed at blocking states from crafting their own regulations for artificial intelligence, saying the burgeoning industry is at risk of being stifled by a patchwork of onerous rules while in a battle with Chinese competitors for supremacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Visual Studio Code adds multi-agent orchestration
item.description: Just-released Visual Studio Code 1.107, the latest version of Microsoft’s popular code editor, introduces multi-agent orchestration, a new feature that allows developers to use GitHub Copilot and custom agents together to speed up software development tasks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.2 as it battles Google’s Gemini 3 for AI model supremacy
item.description: OpenAI has released GPT-5.2, claiming significant gains in the AI model’s ability to complete real-world business tasks to an “expert level” compared to GPT-5.1, released in November.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: California among 19 states suing over Trump’s $100,000 H-1B application fee
item.description: The lawsuit, led by Attorney General Rob Bonta, argues that the fee creates a costly and illegal barrier for employers to use the popular visa program.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Surf the web like it’s 2022 with Slop Evader
item.description: It’s hard to believe that just a few short years ago a video of Will Smith eating spaghetti generated by ModelScope, a text-to-video AI model, was the peak of AI slop.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Broadcom shares tumble after investors seek bigger AI payoff
item.description: Broadcom Inc., a chip company vying with Nvidia Corp. for AI computing revenue, fell by the most in eight months after its sales outlook for the red-hot market failed to meet investors' lofty expectations.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Third-party breach exposes ChatGPT account details
item.description: ChatGPT data breach exposes personal info of users through partner Mixpanel. OpenAI confirms names, emails compromised in security incident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pre-owned electronics are making a comeback
item.description: Electronic gifts are very popular, and in recent years, retailers have been offering significant discounts on smartphones, e-readers, and other electronics labeled as “pre-owned.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Ho ho ho! December’s Patch Tuesday delivers three zero-days
item.description: The December Patch Tuesday update from Microsoft addresses three zero-days (CVE-2025-64671, CVE-2025-54100, and CVE-2025-62221) but includes surprisingly few total patches (just 57).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arc Raiders Cold Snap update release date
item.description: The Arc Raiders Cold Snap update arrives soon. Here's the release date, and what we know about new quests, map conditions, and more.
item.dc:creator: Jake Green, TechRadar

item.title: FinOps Foundation sharpens FOCUS to reduce cloud cost chaos
item.description: A cloud challenge that hampers many organizations is how to normalize billing data across disparate platforms that include multi-cloud and hybrid infrastructure deployments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Investors still doubling down on AI in Taiwan despite 'bubble' fears
item.description: .TWII is up 22% this year to date in line with Nasdaq, but it has a better P/E ratio, easing fears of an AI bubble.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Stop running two architectures
item.description: When I first stepped into enterprise architecture leadership, I expected modernization to unlock capacity and enable growth. We had a roadmap, a cloud architecture we believed in and sponsorship from the business.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: P4 programming: Redefining what’s possible in network infrastructure
item.description: Network engineers have spent decades working within rigid constraints. Your switch vendor decides what protocols you can use, what features you get, and when you get them. Need something custom? You’re out of luck. That’s changing, and P4 is a primary driver.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump signs executive order to block state AI regulations
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to block states from regulating artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Procurement could decide whether AI succeeds or fails in government
item.description: "I like to say you can't move too slow so that you're missing out on things, but you can't move too fast so that you're not ready," said Kathrin Frauscher. The post Procurement could decide whether AI succeeds or fails in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Disney invests $1B in OpenAI in deal to bring characters like Mickey Mouse to Sora AI video tool
item.description: Disney is investing $1 billion in OpenAI to bring characters like Mickey Mouse and Luke Skywalker to the AI company's Sora video tool.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US state attorneys general ask AI giants to fix ‘delusional’ outputs
item.description: After disturbing mental health incidents involving AI chatbots, state attorneys general sent a letter to major AI companies, warning them to fix “delusional outputs” or risk legal action, TechCrunch reported Wednesday. The letter, signed by 42 attorneys general from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Open AI, Microsoft face lawsuit over ChatGPT's alleged role in Connecticut murder-suicide
item.description: The heirs of an 83-year-old Connecticut woman are suing ChatGPT maker OpenAI and its business partner Microsoft for wrongful death.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Matt O'Brien And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: It’s everyone but Meta in a new AI standards group
item.description: It appears Meta has opted to go in a whole new direction in response to this week’s formation by The Linux Foundation of a group called the Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF), designed to help enterprises develop and manage AI agents through a “shared ecosystem of tools, standards and community-driven inn
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ocean warmed by climate change fed intense rainfall and deadly floods in Asia, study finds
item.description: Ocean temperatures warmed by human-caused climate change fed the intense rainfall that triggered deadly floods and landslides across Asia in recent weeks, according to an analysis released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu And Anton L. Delgado, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CISA looks for ‘deep engagement’ with innovators via new platform
item.description: The Industry Engagement Platform is also a step toward CISA requiring just one account to work across the agency's various services and info sharing portals. The post CISA looks for ‘deep engagement’ with innovators via new platform first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: DOJ ordered a sweeping data collection from U.S. attorneys on Trump-era priorities days before Thanksgiving
item.description: "This [data call] did come across as a little bit grueling given the timing with such short turnaround," Ben Penn said. The post DOJ ordered a sweeping data collection from U.S. attorneys on Trump-era priorities days before Thanksgiving first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Foreigners allowed to travel to the US without a visa could soon face new social media screening
item.description: Travelers who are now able to come to the United States without a visa might soon need to provide social media, email, and family history details to the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2
